behaviors.description: Request to external API always includes full taxonomy label list.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is classifier-to-API-client handoff: payload assembly plus dispatch wiring. A fake client that captures outbound payload can catch missing/partial taxonomy. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on model output.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a stub transport/client, call classify(document, taxonomy), and assert outbound request contains every configured label exactly once before send.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected: behavior text is about request content, not real HTTP/network behavior. Agentic rejected: no dependence on model decision quality.

behaviors.description: Out-of-taxonomy API label is mapped to "unknown".
behaviors.justification: Behavior is response-handoff validation at module boundary (API response parser/adapter to public return value). Stubbed API returning an invalid label reproduces the real failure. No real infra or real model judgment needed.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API response with label="nonsense" while taxonomy excludes it; assert classify(...) returns "unknown" and never forwards raw invalid label.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic rejected: behavior checks deterministic guardrail logic, not model correctness.

behaviors.description: Normalization is deterministic for identical raw API responses.
behaviors.justification: This is pure normalization logic on fixed input, with no module interaction required to expose the failure. Real infra and model calls are unnecessary.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with identical raw response fixtures (case/whitespace/punctuation variants already fixed per fixture) and assert identical final label each run.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration rejected: behavior text isolates one local normalization step with identical raw input.

behaviors.description: Repeated live API calls for same input stay consistent unless provider response changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on live model behavior across repeated calls. A mock would hide true non-determinism/drift. Requires real AI API outputs, not just wrapper logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls for the same document/taxonomy, record raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label changes only occur when underlying provider response content changes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration/System rejected by behavior text "repeated live API calls" and consistency tied to provider response changes, which requires real model behavior.

behaviors.description: Configured token budget is enforced before dispatch (truncate/reject policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module pre-dispatch orchestration: tokenizer/budget policy/request builder/client invocation. In-process stubs can verify truncation/rejection and that send is blocked or adjusted.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small budget, run classify with over-budget document using stub client, and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) occurs before send; verify outbound payload token estimate <= budget or no dispatch happened.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected: no real network/OS requirement in behavior text; pre-dispatch policy can be validated in-process.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for typical doc stays within configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Needs real external API/token accounting to catch mismatches between local estimation and provider-reported usage. Stubs would always reflect expected values and miss real provider-side tokenization behavior. This checks infrastructure around the AI call, not label-quality judgments.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API calls for representative typical documents, capture provider-reported usage metadata, and assert each call's reported tokens <= configured budget threshold.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic considered then rejected: behavior text targets token-usage metadata bounds, not model classification quality/decision correctness.

behaviors.description: On rate-limit signal, module waits and retries instead of surfacing immediate error.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in retry orchestration between API adapter, backoff policy, and caller response handling. Simulated rate-limit responses plus controllable clock/backoff in-process can expose missing wait/retry logic. Real model output is irrelevant.
behaviors.test_description: Stub API to return rate-limit error then success; run classify with fake clock/backoff and assert wait occurred, retry count/policy was honored, and final result returned without propagating initial error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected: real network conditions are not required to validate retry state machine correctness.
